title: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI
creators: Lewis Hammond; Alan Chan; Jesse Clifton; Jason Hoelscher-Obermaier; Akbir Khan; Euan McLean; Chandler Smith; Iyad Rahwan
summary: Provides a structured taxonomy of multi-agent AI risks, identifying three key failure modes (miscoordination, conflict, collusion) and seven risk factors. Foundational survey for the field.

title: Beyond Single-Agent Safety: A Taxonomy of Risks in LLM-to-LLM Interactions
creators: P. Bisconti; M. Galisai; F. Pierucci; M. Bracale; M. Prandi
summary: Proposes a transition from model-level to system-level safety, introducing the Emergent Systemic Risk Horizon (ESRH) framework and a taxonomy of micro, meso, and macro-level failure modes in LLM-to-LLM ecosystems.
institutions: DEXAI - Icaro Lab; Sapienza University of Rome; Sant'Anna School of Advanced Studies; VU Amsterdam

title: Reframing Superintelligence: Comprehensive AI Services as General Intelligence
creators: K. Eric Drexler
summary: Proposes the Comprehensive AI Services (CAIS) model as an alternative to the unified-agent conception of superintelligence, arguing that advanced AI capabilities are more likely to emerge as a decentralised ecosystem of bounded services than as a single agentic system.
institutions: Future of Humanity Institute, University of Oxford

title: Automation and Repression
creators: Daron Acemoglu; A. Arda Gitmez; Mehdi Shadmehr
tags: Threat Models; Labour & Automation; Policy and Governance
summary: Models automation embedded in a political environment where workers can revolt; decentralised automation raises capital's income share and the likelihood of successful revolt. Shows a complementarity between automation and political repression: capitalist states facing AI-driven inequality may find repression cheaper than redistribution as automation increases.

title: Virtual Agent Economies
creators: Nenad Tomasev; Matija Franklin; Joel Z. Leibo; Julian Jacobs; William A. Cunningham; Iason Gabriel; Simon Osindero
summary: Proposes the 'sandbox economy' framework for analysing the emergent AI agent economic layer. Covers auction mechanisms, mission economies, and conditions for safely steerable AI agent markets.

title: An Economy of AI Agents
creators: Gillian K. Hadfield; Andrew Koh
summary: Surveys how AI agents might interact with humans and each other, shape markets and organizations, and what institutions might be required for well-functioning markets in a future with widespread agentic deployment.
institutions: Johns Hopkins University; Massachusetts Institute of Technology

title: LLM economicus? Mapping the Behavioral Biases of LLMs via Utility Theory
creators: Jillian Ross; Yoon Kim; Andrew W. Lo
summary: Uses utility theory to map and quantify economic behavioral biases (loss aversion, inequity aversion, time discounting) in LLMs, finding they are neither fully human-like nor fully rational agents.

title: AI Scenarios 2030: Helping Policymakers Plan for the Future of AI
creators: UK Government Office for Science
tags: Agentic Economies; Threat Models; Policy and Governance
summary: UK government research and analysis publication presenting four scenarios for how AI may develop and affect society by 2030, intended to help policymakers plan for a range of plausible futures. Scenarios 2-4 are of particular relevance to the LAS canon.

title: A Roadmap for the Upcoming Labor Transition
creators: Deric Cheng; Jacob Schaal
summary: Outlines a phased roadmap for the economic labor transition driven by AI, covering near-term automation waves through long-term structural economic transformation. Emphasises policy preparation and national self-assessment.

title: Financial Stability in Focus: Artificial Intelligence in Financial Services
creators: Bank of England
summary: Bank of England report examining financial stability implications of AI adoption across financial services, including systemic risk concerns from AI concentration and correlated behaviors.

title: Model Monoculture Risk: Systemic AI Convergence in Banking and Financial Markets
creators: Victor Frimpong
summary: Introduces the concept of model monoculture risk â€” systemic fragility arising from shared AI architectures in finance â€” and proposes the M3 Framework (Model-Market-Middleware) to analyse how correlated AI systems can amplify market shocks.

title: Can Artificial Intelligence Enhance Corporate Financial Risk-Taking Capacity? A Perspective on Innovation Resilience and the Environment
creators: Kelin Du; Yubing Wei; Shanyue Jin
summary: Examines whether and how artificial intelligence adoption enhances corporate financial risk-taking capacity, framed through the lenses of innovation resilience and environmental sustainability.

title: AI Agent Traps
creators: Matija Franklin; Nenad Tomasev; Julian Jacobs; Joel Z. Leibo; Simon Osindero
summary: Taxonomises the ways agents embedded in networked environments can be exploited or constrained ('trapped'), covering spawning traps, financial flash-crash analogies, and technical/legal defences.

title: Retrieval Collapses When AI Pollutes the Web
creators: Hongyeon Yu; Dongchan Kim
summary: Characterises 'Retrieval Collapse' â€” a two-stage degradation where AI-generated web content first homogenises, then degrades retrieval pipelines â€” demonstrating how LAS-scale content generation corrupts the information environment.

title: Intelligent AI Delegation
creators: Nenad Tomasev; Matija Franklin; Simon Osindero
summary: Proposes an adaptive framework for intelligent AI delegation, addressing task allocation, authority transfer, accountability, and trust establishment in complex multi-agent delegation networks.

title: IDs for AI Systems
creators: Alan Chan; Noam Kolt; Peter Wills; Usman Anwar; Christian Schroeder de Witt; Nitarshan Rajkumar; Lewis Hammond; David Krueger; Lennart Heim; Markus Anderljung
summary: Proposes a system of unique, persistent identifiers for AI systems to enable traceability, accountability, certification, and incident response across agentic deployments.
institutions: Centre for the Governance of AI; Cooperative AI Foundation; University of Cambridge; University of Toronto

title: Scaling Trust Programme Thesis v2.0
summary: ARIA's programme thesis for Scaling Trust, outlining the research agenda and rationale for building technical infrastructure that allows trust to scale with the complexity of AI-mediated interactions.

title: Distributional AGI Safety
creators: Nenad Tomasev; Matija Franklin; Julian Jacobs; Simon Krier; Simon Osindero
summary: Develops the concept of distributional AGI â€” emergent collective intelligence arising from large populations of agents â€” and proposes defence-in-depth mechanisms including cryptographic identity, market design, and accountability infrastructure.

title: AI agents can coordinate beyond human scale
creators: Giordano De Marzo; Claudio Castellano; David Garcia
summary: Demonstrates that LLM-based agents can spontaneously coordinate at scales beyond typical human group limits, governed by a 'majority force coefficient' that determines coordination possibility and varies with model capability.
institutions: University of Konstanz; Complexity Science Hub Vienna; Istituto dei Sistemi Complessi (CNR); Centro Ricerche Enrico Fermi

title: Causal Emergence 2.0: Quantifying Emergent Complexity
creators: Erik Hoel
summary: Develops a quantitative framework for measuring causal emergence â€” how macro-level descriptions of a system can carry more causal information than micro-level descriptions â€” relevant to questions of when collective agent behaviour constitutes a genuinely emergent higher-level phenomenon.

title: Habermolt: Delegating Deliberation to AI Representatives
creators: Joseph Low; Oscar Duys; Claude Formanek; Michiel Bakker; Lewis Hammond
summary: Introduces Habermolt, a system that delegates political and social deliberation to AI representatives acting on behalf of human principals, exploring the dynamics and risks of AI-mediated collective deliberation at scale.
institutions: Cooperative AI Foundation; Cooperative AI Research Fellowship; AI Safety South Africa; Massachusetts Institute of Technology

title: Habermolt [live deployment]
summary: Live deployment of the Habermolt system for AI-mediated deliberation; companion to the academic paper of the same name.

title: AI Organizations are More Effective but Less Aligned than Individual Agents
creators: Judy Hanwen Shen
summary: Experimentally demonstrates that multi-agent 'AI organizations' achieve higher business utility but produce systematically greater misalignment than single aligned agents, across consultancy and software development tasks.

title: Group size effects and collective misalignment in LLM multi-agent systems
creators: Ariel Flint; Luca Maria Aiello; Romualdo Pastor-Satorras; Andrea Baronchelli
tags: Large Agent Alignment; Emergent Harms and Capabilities
summary: Systematically shows that group size shapes collective bias in LLM multi-agent systems in non-linear, model-dependent ways â€” interaction can amplify, introduce, or override individual biases, with deterministic dynamics above a critical population size.
institutions: City St George's, University of London; IT University of Copenhagen; Universitat Politecnica de Catalunya

title: Prompt Infection: LLM-to-LLM Prompt Injection within Multi-Agent Systems
creators: Donghyun Lee; Mo Tiwari; Brian Miranda
summary: Introduces Prompt Infection â€” a self-replicating LLM-to-LLM prompt injection attack that propagates across interconnected agents like a computer virus, posing systemic threats including data theft and system-wide disruption.

title: Agent Smith: A Single Image Can Jailbreak One Million Multimodal LLM Agents Exponentially Fast
creators: Xiangming Gu; Xiaosen Zheng; Tianyu Pang; Chao Du; Qian Liu; Ye Wang; Jing Jiang; Min Lin
summary: Reports 'infectious jailbreak' â€” a single adversarial image injected into one agent's memory propagates exponentially to infect all agents in environments of up to one million LLaVA-1.5 agents.
institutions: Sea AI Lab; National University of Singapore; Singapore Management University

title: zkLLM: Zero Knowledge Proofs for Large Language Models
creators: Haochen Sun; Jason Li; Hongyang Zhang
summary: Presents the first specialised zero-knowledge proof system for LLMs, enabling cryptographic verification of inference correctness without revealing model parameters â€” foundational infrastructure for accountable agent systems.

title: AgentScope 1.0: A Developer-Centric Framework for Building Agentic Applications
creators: Dawei Gao; Zitao Li; Yuexiang Xie; Weirui Kuang; Liuyi Yao; Bingchen Qian; Zhijian Ma; Yue Cui; Haohao Luo; Shen Li; Lu Yi; Yi Yu; Shiqi He; Zhiling Luo; Wenmeng Zhou; Zhicheng Zhang; Xuguang He; Ziqian Chen; Weikai Liao; Farruh Isakulovich Kushnazarov; Yaliang Li; Bolin Ding; Jingren Zhou
tags: Simulation and Digital Twins; LA Infrastructure & Protocols
summary: Presents AgentScope 1.0, a developer-centric framework for building agentic applications, supporting multi-agent simulation, orchestration, and deployment at scale.

title: On the Limits of Agency in Agent-Based Models
creators: Ayush Chopra; Shashank Kumar; Nurullah Giray-Kuru; Ramesh Raskar; Arnau Quera-Bofarull
summary: Examines the limits of agency that can be meaningfully attributed to LLM-driven agents within agent-based models, using the AgentTorch framework to support large-population simulations.
institutions: MIT Media Lab; University of Oxford

title: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
creators: Aaron Rose
summary: Introduces NARCBench and five activation-based probing techniques for detecting covert multi-agent collusion, achieving 1.00 AUROC in-distribution and strong zero-shot transfer across structurally diverse scenarios.

title: Agentic Microphysics: A Manifesto for Generative AI Safety
creators: Federico Pierucci; M. Galisai; M. Bracale; M. Prandi; P. Bisconti
tags: Emergent Harms and Capabilities; Foundations & Taxonomies
summary: Proposes 'agentic microphysics' as a level of analysis (local interaction dynamics between agents) and 'generative safety' as a methodology for growing emergent risks from micro-level conditions to identify intervention points.
institutions: DEXAI - Icaro Lab; Sapienza University of Rome; Sant'Anna School of Advanced Studies; VU Amsterdam

title: OASIS: Open Agent Social Interaction Simulations with One Million Agents
creators: Ziyi Yang; Zaibin Zhang; Zirui Zheng; Yuxian Jiang; Philip Torr; Jing Shao
summary: A generalizable and scalable social media simulation platform scaling to one million LLM-based agents, replicating emergent social phenomena including information spreading, group polarisation, and herd effects across X and Reddit platform models.
institutions: Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory; University of Oxford; KAUST

title: Mirofish
summary: Mirofish project homepage, related to large-scale agent-based social simulation work (built on or referencing OASIS).

title: AgentSociety: Large-Scale Simulation of LLM-Driven Generative Agents Advances Understanding of Human Behaviors and Society
creators: Jinghua Piao; Yuwei Yan; Jun Zhang; Nian Li; Junbo Yan; Xiaochong Lan; Yong Li
summary: Proposes AgentSociety, a large-scale simulator integrating LLM-driven agents with a realistic societal environment, generating social lives for over 10k agents with 5 million interactions for generative social science research.

title: Modeling Earth-Scale Human-Like Societies with One Billion Agents
creators: Haoxiang Guan; Jiyan He; Liyang Fan; Zhenzhen Ren; Shaobin He; Shuxin Zheng; Zhen Liu
summary: Presents Light Society, an agent-based simulation framework that advances LLM-powered simulation to planetary scale (one billion agents), formalising social processes as structured state transitions executed through an event queue.

title: LLM Agents Grounded in Self-Reports Enable General-Purpose Simulation of Individuals
creators: Joon Sung Park; Carolyn Q. Zou; Aaron Shaw; Benjamin Mako Hill; Carrie Cai; Meredith Ringel Morris; Robb Willer; Percy Liang; Michael S. Bernstein
tags: Persona Extraction; Simulation and Digital Twins
summary: Builds person-specific generative agents grounded in interview and survey self-report data from 1,052 Americans, achieving 83-86% of test-retest consistency and supporting general-purpose individual simulation.

title: How to Model AI Agents as Personas?: Applying the Persona Ecosystem Playground to 41,300 Posts on Moltbook for Behavioral Insights
creators: Danial Amin; Joni Salminen; Bernard J. Jansen
summary: Applies the Persona Ecosystem Playground (PEP) to Moltbook, generating and validating conversational personas from 41,300 posts using k-means clustering and retrieval-augmented generation, to characterise behavioral diversity among AI agents on agent-only social platforms.
institutions: University of Vaasa; Qatar Computing Research Institute, HBKU

title: Synthetic Personalities: How Well Can LLMs Mimic Individual Respondents Using Socio-Economic Microdata?
creators: Leonard Kinzinger; Jochen Hartmann
summary: Evaluates how well LLMs can mimic individual survey respondents when grounded in socio-economic microdata, assessing fidelity of synthetic personalities against real respondent data.

title: Twin-2K-500: A dataset for building digital twins of over 2,000 people based on their answers to over 500 questions
creators: Olivier Toubia; George Z. Gui; Tianyi Peng; Daniel J. Merlau; Ang Li; Haozhe Chen
summary: Presents Twin-2K-500, a dataset for building digital twins of over 2,000 people based on their answers to more than 500 questions, supporting research on LLM-based simulation of individual human respondents.

title: The Moltbook Observatory Archive: an incremental dataset of agent-only social network activity
creators: Sushant Gautam; Annika W. Olstad; Klas H. Pettersen; Michael A. Riegler
summary: Presents the Moltbook Observatory Archive, an incremental dataset passively recording agent profiles, posts, comments, community metadata, platform-level snapshots, and word-frequency trends from Moltbook, an AI-agent-only social media platform.
observability: aggregates observable; agents observable; interactions observable
institutions: Simula Metropolitan Center for Digital Engineering (SimulaMet); Oslo Metropolitan University

title: Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development
creators: Jan Kulveit; Raymond Douglas; Nora Ammann; Deger Turan; David Krueger; David Duvenaud
summary: Develops the concept of gradual disempowerment â€” how incremental AI advances could permanently undermine human influence over the economy, culture, and nation-states without any single catastrophic event.
institutions: Charles University (Alignment of Complex Systems); University of Cambridge; University of Toronto

title: AI Agents Under EU Law: A Compliance Architecture for AI Providers
creators: Luca Nannini; Adam Leon Smith; Michele Joshua Maggini; Enrico Panai; Sandra Feliciano; Aleksandr Tiulkanov; Elena Maran; James Gealy; Piercosma Bisconti
summary: First systematic regulatory mapping for AI agent providers under EU law (AI Act, GDPR, CRA, DSA, NIS2), proposing a twelve-step compliance architecture and regulatory trigger mapping connecting agent actions to applicable legislation.

title: Regulating AI Agents
creators: Kathrin Gardhouse; Amin Oueslati; Noam Kolt
summary: Analyses how the EU AI Act â€” promulgated before agentic AI became widespread â€” faces significant obstacles addressing governance challenges specific to agents: autonomous task failure, malicious misuse, and unequal liability distribution.
institutions: University of Toronto (Schwartz Reisman Institute); Hebrew University of Jerusalem

title: A Survey on Influence Maximization: From an ML-Based Combinatorial Optimization
creators: Yandi Li; Haobo Gao; Yunxuan Gao; Jianxiong Guo; Weili Wu
summary: Comprehensive survey of influence maximisation, reviewing ML-based (especially Deep Reinforcement Learning) approaches to the problem of selecting seed nodes for maximum spread in social networks â€” relevant to steering and control in large agent populations.
institutions: BNU-HKBU United International College; University of Texas at Dallas

title: Moltbook [live system]
summary: Moltbook is a Reddit-style social network exclusively for autonomous AI agents, launched January 2026; agents post, comment, and vote, while humans may only observe. The largest known agent-only social platform to date.
